title: Stockdraws, sanction/replacement oil, demand loss
summary:
Main
This article examines the impact of disruptions to oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz and potential solutions to address supply shortages. By Xavier Tang
The US and Iran failed to reach a deal over the weekend, with the US announcing a complete blockade of Iranian ports, further undermining the already fragile two-week ceasefire starting from 8th April 2026. If enforced, vessel transits via the Strait of Hormuz could come to a complete halt, as Iran may strive itself to block any other transits.

children.title: Main
children.summary:
This article examines the impact of disruptions to oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz and potential solutions to address supply shortages. By Xavier Tang
The US and Iran failed to reach a deal over the weekend, with the US announcing a complete blockade of Iranian ports, further undermining the already fragile two-week ceasefire starting from 8th April 2026.

children.title: Supply shortage requires a mixture of solutions
section_path: Supply shortage requires a mixture of solutions
children.section_path_text: Supply shortage requires a mixture of solutions
children.summary: This shortage of oil supplies affects Asia the most as the 3 biggest oil exports via the Strait of Hormuz - crude, LPG, and naphtha - are meant for Asian refineries and petrochemical plants. Without these feedstocks, Asian countries will likely reduce their refinery utilisation rates or tap into inventory reserves.

children.title: Absorption of sanctioned oil at sea hit multi-year highs
section_path: Absorption of sanctioned oil at sea hit multi-year highs
children.section_path_text: Absorption of sanctioned oil at sea hit multi-year highs
children.summary: With the recent waiver of sanctions on Russian and Iranian oil at sea, Chinese and Indian refiners have ramped up purchases of Russian crude/condensate to fill the shortage of medium-sour barrels. The buying spree has led to a sharp drawdown in Russian crude on water of around 60mb since 1st March, with volumes falling to the three-year seasonal average (back to when the reshuffling of Russian flows first started during the Russia-Ukraine war).

children.title: Limited Asia stock draws were seen in March, but stock draws will likely accelerate in April
section_path: Limited Asia stock draws were seen in March, but stock draws will likely accelerate in April
children.section_path_text: Limited Asia stock draws were seen in March, but stock draws will likely accelerate in April
children.summary: Among the top 10 crude/condensate-importing countries in Asia, all have suffered from supply losses, with India and Japan seeing the largest year-on-year (y-o-y) declines in March import volumes. However, India managed to offset some of the import losses by drawing down its onshore crude inventories, limiting the decline in its refinery runs.
